SAN JOSE TOUCHLESS CARWASH GONZALEZ-HERNANDEZ, YSAEL ARAUJO-CATALAN, EZEQUIEL DOES 1 TO 25 SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR JAN-25-2017 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  OCT-24-2016 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         JAN-03-2017

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE TO PROPER COURT IN COUNTY OF DEFEDANTS' RESIDENCE (TRANSACTION ID # 100005030) FILED BY DEFENDANT FULL SERVICE CARWASH, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FULL SERVICE CARWASH, INC.'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS GRANTED. THIS CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY PURSUANT TO CCP 396B. FULL SERVICE SHALL PAY ALL TRANSFER FEES WITHIN TEN DAYS BECAUSE OF ITS ERRONEOUS LISTING OF A SAN FRANCISCO ADDRESS IN ITS SECRETARY OF STATE FILINGS. FULL SERVICE HAS SHOWN THAT VENUE IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY IS IMPROPER BECAUSE NONE OF THE DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN SAN FRANCISCO AND THE ALLEGED INJURY DID NOT OCCUR IN THIS COUNTY. WHILE FULL SERVICE LISTED A SAN FRANCISCO ADDRESS IN ITS SECRETARY OF STATE FILINGS AT THE TIME PLAINTIFFS FILED THIS LAWSUIT, FULL SERVICE HAS SHOWN THAT THE SAN FRANCISCO ADDRESS WAS INCORRECT AND THE SECRETARY OF STATE FILING HAS BEEN CHANGED. ROSAS V. SUPERIOR COURT (1994) 25 CAL.APP.4TH 671, 677 SUGGESTS THAT A PARTY CAN AFFECT VENUE BY CHANGING THE ADDRESS IT DESIGNATED ON PUBLIC FILINGS. THE INTERESTS OF JUSTICE ARE SERVED BY TAKING INTO ACCOUNT THAT SAN JOSE, NOT SAN FRANCISCO, IS THE TRUE LOCATION OF FULL SERVICE'S PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS. BECAUSE THE ALLEGED INJURY TOOK PLACE IN SANTA CLARA COUNTY AND ALL DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN THAT COUNTY, THE CASE WILL BE TRANSFERRED TO THAT COUNTY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Change Of Venue To Proper Court In County Of Defendants Residence
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Full Service Carwash, Inc.'s motion for change of venue is granted. This case is transferred to Santa Clara County pursuant to CCP 396b. Full Service shall pay all transfer fees within ten days because of its erroneous listing of a San Francisco address in its Secretary of State filings. Full Service has shown that venue in San Francisco County is improper because none of the defendants reside in San Francisco and the alleged injury did not occur in this county. While Full Service listed a San Francisco address in its Secretary of State filings at the time plaintiffs filed this lawsuit, Full Service has shown that the San Francisco address was incorrect and the Secretary of State filing has been changed. Rosas v. Superior Court (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 671, 677 suggests that a party can affect venue by changing the address it designated on public filings. The interests of justice are served by taking into account that San Jose, not San Francisco, is the true location of Full Service's principal place of business. Because the alleged injury took place in Santa Clara County and all defendants reside in that county, the case will be transferred to that county. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
